title: Overall Composition Quality Across MRSA Variants
caption: Bar chart comparing the primary metric for all 10 model variants. This is the main results figure showing how the full Adaptive MRSA design compares against vanilla FreeCustom and targeted removals of similarity scaling, conflict routing, scheduling, and residual boosting components.
data_source: Condition-level primary_metric for all conditions: VanillaFreeCustomMRSA, ForegroundOnlySimilarityWithoutConflictRouting, BackgroundBlindConflictRoutingWithoutDeconfounding, ForegroundDeconfoundedConflictRoutedMRSA, StaticExclusivityWithoutThreePhaseSchedule, LateOnlySeparationWithoutEarlyOwnershipStabilization, EarlyOverSeparationScheduledMRSA, DenseUncappedResidualBoostedMRSA, SharedFeatureSuppressionWithoutResidualBoost, SparseCappedResidualBoostedMRSA

title: Performance Under Matched vs. Mismatched Background Context
caption: Grouped bars compare each method under matched-background/shared-context and mismatched-background/clean-context evaluation slices where available. This figure tests whether Adaptive MRSA improves multi-concept composition robustly when contextual cues either align with or conflict with the target concepts.
data_source: Per-condition primary_metric for matched_background_or_shared_context/11 and mismatched_background_or_clean_context/11 for conditions with available subgroup metrics: VanillaFreeCustomMRSA, ForegroundOnlySimilarityWithoutConflictRouting, BackgroundBlindConflictRoutingWithoutDeconfounding, ForegroundDeconfoundedConflictRoutedMRSA, StaticExclusivityWithoutThreePhaseSchedule

title: Ablation of Similarity, Conflict Routing, Scheduling, and Residual Design
caption: Grouped ablation figure organizing variants by the component they alter: similarity estimation, conflict routing/deconfounding, phase scheduling, and residual boost design. It isolates which parts of MRSA contribute most strongly to composition quality and clarifies whether gains arise from the adaptive attention mechanism itself or from supporting training/aggregation choices.
data_source: Primary metric grouped by ablation family using conditions: VanillaFreeCustomMRSA; ForegroundOnlySimilarityWithoutConflictRouting, BackgroundBlindConflictRoutingWithoutDeconfounding, ForegroundDeconfoundedConflictRoutedMRSA; StaticExclusivityWithoutThreePhaseSchedule, LateOnlySeparationWithoutEarlyOwnershipStabilization, EarlyOverSeparationScheduledMRSA; DenseUncappedResidualBoostedMRSA, SharedFeatureSuppressionWithoutResidualBoost, SparseCappedResidualBoostedMRSA

title: Seed-to-Seed Stability of MRSA Variants
caption: Distribution plot of the primary metric across random seeds for the principal methods and key ablations. This figure assesses whether Adaptive MRSA improves not only average performance but also training/inference stability relative to vanilla and partially disabled variants.
data_source: Per-seed primary_metric distributions for all conditions, or at minimum for VanillaFreeCustomMRSA, ForegroundDeconfoundedConflictRoutedMRSA, LateOnlySeparationWithoutEarlyOwnershipStabilization, EarlyOverSeparationScheduledMRSA, DenseUncappedResidualBoostedMRSA, SparseCappedResidualBoostedMRSA
